var_functions.query_db:12.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

# Extract year from date strings for filtering
import re

def extract_year(date_str):
    if not date_str:
        return None
    # Look for 4-digit year pattern
    match = re.search(r'(\d{4})', str(date_str))
    if match:
        return int(match.group(1))
    return None

# Test the extract_year function

var_functions.query_db:12.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
# Load all data
import json
import re

users_2016 = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:24']

# Read the full business data file
business_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:26']
with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Extract user IDs
user_ids_2016 = [u['user_id'] for u in users_2016]

# Create a map from business_id to business document
business_map = {b['business_id']: b for b in businesses}

# Function to extract year from date string
def extract_year(date_str):
    if not date_str or date_str == 'None':
        return None
    match = re.search(r'(\d{4})', str(date_str))
    if match:
        return int(match.group(1))
    return None

# Function to extract categories from business description
def extract_categories(description):
    if not description or description == 'None':
        return []
    
    # Look for patterns like "services in X, Y, and Z" or "categories of 'X, Y'"
    # Common patterns:
    # "services in A, B, C, and D."
    # "category of 'X, Y'."
    # "services including A, B, and C."
    
    desc = str(description)
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern 1: "services in" or "services including" followed by list
    patterns = [
        r'services in ([^.]+)',
        r'services including ([^.]+)',
        r'category of [\'"]([^\'"]+)[\'"]',
        r'categories of ([^.]+)',
        r'offers a range of ([^.]+)',
        r'offers ([^.]+ services)'
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        matches = re.findall(pattern, desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        for match in matches:
            # Split by commas and clean up
            parts = re.split(r',\s*and\s*|,\s*|and\s*|\s*&\s*', match)
            for part in parts:
                part = part.strip().rstrip('.').rstrip('"').rstrip("'")
                if part and len(part) < 100:  # Avoid capturing long sentences
                    categories.append(part)
    
    # Pattern 2: if description contains "Located at..." then extract after that
    if 'Located at' in desc:
        # Extract everything after the location part
        after_location = re.split(r'Located at [^.,]+[.,]\s*', desc)
        if len(after_location) > 1:
            text = after_location[1]
            # Look for "offers" or "provides" or "including"
            offer_matches = re.findall(r'(?:offers|provides|including) ([^.]+)', text)
            for match in offer_matches:
                parts = re.split(r',\s*and\s*|,\s*|and\s*|\s*&\s*', match)
                for part in parts:
                    part = part.strip().rstrip('.').rstrip('in').strip()
                    if part and len(part) < 100:
                        categories.append(part)
    
    return list(set(categories))  # Remove duplicates

# Test category extraction
sample_business = businesses[0]
sample_cats = extract_categories(sample_business.get('description', ''))
print('Sample business:', sample_business.get('name'))
print('Categories found:', sample_cats)

# Filter reviews from 2016 onwards by users who registered in 2016
filtered_reviews = []
for review in reviews:
    user_id = review.get('user_id')
    date_str = review.get('date')
    
    if user_id in user_ids_2016:
        year = extract_year(date_str)
        if year and year >= 2016:
            filtered_reviews.append(review)

print('Total reviews from 2016+ by 2016-registered users:', len(filtered_reviews))

# Count reviews per category
category_review_counts = {}

for review in filtered_reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    
    # Convert business_ref to business_id by changing prefix
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        business = business_map[business_id]
        description = business.get('description', '')
        categories = extract_categories(description)
        
        for category in categories:
            category_review_counts[category] = category_review_counts.get(category, 0) + 1

# Sort categories by review count
top_categories = sorted(category_review_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

result = {
    '2016_user_count': len(user_ids_2016),
    'filtered_review_count': len(filtered_reviews),
    'top_categories': top_categories[:10],  # Top 10 for now
    'total_categories_found': len(category_review_counts)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

print('Total reviews from 2016+ by 2016-registered users:', len(filtered_reviews))

# Count reviews per category
category_review_counts = {}

for review in filtered_reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    
    # Convert business_ref to business_id by changing prefix
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        business = business_map[business_id]
        description = business.get('description', '')
        categories = extract_categories(description)
        
        for category in categories:
            category_review_counts[category] = category_review_counts.get(category, 0) + 1

# Sort categories by review count
top_categories = sorted(category_review_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

result = {
    '2016_user_count': len(user_ids_2016),
    'filtered_review_count': len(filtered_reviews),
    'top_categories': top_categories[:10],  # Top 10 for now
    'total_categories_found': len(category_review_counts)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:12.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:12.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

import json
import re

# Load all data
businesses_all = locals()['var_functions.query_db:52']
users_2016 = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
reviews_all = locals()['var_functions.query_db:24']

# Setup
user_ids_2016 = [u['user_id'] for u in users_2016]
business_map = {b['business_id']: b for b in businesses_all}

# Extract year from date string
def extract_year(date_str):
    if not date_str:
        return None
    match = re.search(r'(\d{4})', str(date_str))
    return int(match.group(1)) if match else None

# Extract categories from business description
def extract_categories(description):
    if not description or description == 'None':
        return []
    
    desc = str(description)
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern 1: services in Category1, Category2, and Category3
    match = re.search(r'services in ([^.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        items = re.split(r',\s*and\s*|,\s*|\s+and\s+', text)
        for item in items:
            cleaned = item.strip().rstrip('.').rstrip('"').strip("'")
            if cleaned and len(cleaned) > 2:
                categories.append(cleaned)
    
    # Pattern 2: services including Category1, Category2
    match = re.search(r'services,? including ([^.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        items = re.split(r',\s*and\s*|,\s*|\s+and\s+', text)
        for item in items:
            cleaned = item.strip().rstrip('.').rstrip('"').strip("'")
            if cleaned and len(cleaned) > 2:
                categories.append(cleaned)
    
    # Pattern 3: category of 'Category1, Category2'
    match = re.search(r"category of ['"]([^'"]+)["']", desc, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        text = match.group(1)
        items = re.split(r',\s*', text)
        for item in items:
            cleaned = item.strip()
            if cleaned:
                categories.append(cleaned)
    
    # Remove duplicates and clean
    unique_cats = []
    seen = set()
    for cat in categories:
        cat_lower = cat.lower()
        if cat_lower not in seen and cat_lower != 'services':
            seen.add(cat_lower)
            unique_cats.append(cat)
    
    return unique_cats

# Sample extraction
samples = []
for i in range(min(5, len(businesses_all))):
    biz = businesses_all[i]
    cats = extract_categories(biz.get('description', ''))
    samples.append({'business': biz['name'], 'cats': cats})

# Filter reviews
filtered_reviews = []
for review in reviews_all:
    user_id = review.get('user_id')
    if user_id and user_id in user_ids_2016:
        year = extract_year(review.get('date', ''))
        if year and year >= 2016:
            filtered_reviews.append(review)

# Count reviews by category
category_counts = {}
for review in filtered_reviews:
    business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if business_ref:
        business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
        if business_id in business_map:
            business = business_map[business_id]
            cats = extract_categories(business.get('description', ''))
            for cat in cats:
                category_counts[cat] = category_counts.get(cat, 0) + 1

# Get top 5
top_5 = sorted(category_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5]

# Result
result = {
    'total_2016_users': len(user_ids_2016),
    'filtered_reviews': len(filtered_reviews),
    'top_5_categories': top_5,
    'samples': samples
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load all data
businesses_all = locals()['var_functions.query_db:52']
users_2016 = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
reviews_all = locals()['var_functions.query_db:24']

# Setup
user_ids_2016 = [u['user_id'] for u in users_2016]
business_map = {}
for b in businesses_all:
    business_map[b['business_id']] = b

# Extract year from date
def get_year(date_str):
    if not date_str:
        return None
    match = re.search(r'(\d{4})', str(date_str))
    return int(match.group(1)) if match else None

# Extract categories from description - IMPROVED VERSION
def extract_categories(desc):
    if not desc or desc == 'None':
        return []
    
    text = str(desc)
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern 1: "services in Category1, Category2, and Category3"
    match = re.search(r'services in ([^.]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        cat_text = match.group(1)
        # Split by comma first, then handle 'and'
        items = cat_text.split(',')
        for item in items:
            item = item.strip()
            # Remove 'and ' from the last item if present
            if item.startswith('and '):
                item = item[4:]
            if item.startswith('and'):
                item = item[3:]
            item = item.rstrip('.').strip('"').strip("'")
            if item and len(item) > 2:
                categories.append(item)
    
    # Pattern 2: "services including Category1, Category2, and Category3"
    match = re.search(r'services,? including ([^.]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        cat_text = match.group(1)
        items = cat_text.split(',')
        for item in items:
            item = item.strip()
            if item.startswith('and '):
                item = item[4:]
            if item.startswith('and'):
                item = item[3:]
            item = item.rstrip('.').strip('"').strip("'")
            if item and len(item) > 2:
                categories.append(item)
    
    # Pattern 3: "category of 'Category1, Category2'"
    match = re.search(r"category of ['\"]([^'\"]+)[\"']", text, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        cat_text = match.group(1)
        items = cat_text.split(',')
        for item in items:
            item = item.strip()
            item = item.rstrip('.').strip('"').strip("'")
            if item and len(item) > 2:
                categories.append(item)
    
    # Clean up: remove duplicates (case-insensitive), keep original case
    seen = set()
    cleaned_categories = []
    for cat in categories:
        cat_lower = cat.lower()
        if cat_lower not in seen:
            seen.add(cat_lower)
            cleaned_categories.append(cat)
    
    return cleaned_categories

# Test extractions
print('Testing category extraction:')
samples = []
for i in range(min(5, len(businesses_all))):
    biz = businesses_all[i]
    cats = extract_categories(biz.get('description', ''))
    samples.append({'business': biz['name'], 'categories': cats})
    print(f"Business: {biz['name']}")
    print(f"Categories: {cats}")
    print('---')

# Filter reviews: 2016-registered users, reviews from 2016 onwards
category_review_counts = {}
for review in reviews_all:
    user_id = review.get('user_id')
    if user_id and user_id in user_ids_2016:
        year = get_year(review.get('date'))
        if year and year >= 2016:
            business_ref = review.get('business_ref')
            if business_ref:
                business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
                if business_id in business_map:
                    business = business_map[business_id]
                    cats = extract_categories(business.get('description', ''))
                    for cat in cats:
                        # Clean up the category name
                        cat_clean = cat.strip()
                        if cat_clean:
                            category_review_counts[cat_clean] = category_review_counts.get(cat_clean, 0) + 1

# Get top 5 categories
top_5 = sorted(category_review_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5]

print('Top 5 categories:')
for cat, count in top_5:
    print(f"{cat}: {count}")

result = {
    'top_5_business_categories': top_5,
    'total_filtered_reviews': sum(category_review_counts.values()),
    'total_unique_categories': len(category_review_counts),
    'sample_extractions': samples
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

users_2016 = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
reviews_all = locals()['var_functions.query_db:24']

user_ids_2016 = [u['user_id'] for u in users_2016]
business_map = {}
for b in businesses_all:
    business_map[b['business_id']] = b

# Extract year from date
def get_year(date_str):
    if not date_str:
        return None
    match = re.search(r'(\d{4})', str(date_str))
    return int(match.group(1)) if match else None

# Extract categories from description - IMPROVED VERSION
def extract_categories(desc):
    if not desc or desc == 'None':
        return []
    
    text = str(desc)
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern 1: "services in Category1, Category2, and Category3"
    match = re.search(r'services in ([^.]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        cat_text = match.group(1)
        # Split by comma first, then handle 'and'
        items = cat_text.split(',')
        for item in items:
            item = item.strip()
            # Remove 'and ' from the last item if present
            if item.startswith('and '):
                item = item[4:]
            if item.startswith('and'):
                item = item[3:]
            item = item.rstrip('.').strip('"').strip("'")
            if item and len(item) > 2:
                categories.append(item)
    
    # Pattern 2: "services including Category1, Category2, and Category3"
    match = re.search(r'services,? including ([^.]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        cat_text = match.group(1)
        items = cat_text.split(',')
        for item in items:
            item = item.strip()
            if item.startswith('and '):
                item = item[4:]
            if item.startswith('and'):
                item = item[3:]
            item = item.rstrip('.').strip('"').strip("'")
            if item and len(item) > 2:
                categories.append(item)
    
    # Pattern 3: "category of 'Category1, Category2'"
    match = re.search(r"category of ['\"]([^'\"]+)[\"']", text, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        cat_text = match.group(1)
        items = cat_text.split(',')
        for item in items:
            item = item.strip()
            item = item.rstrip('.').strip('"').strip("'")
            if item and len(item) > 2:
                categories.append(item)
    
    # Clean up: remove duplicates (case-insensitive), keep original case
    seen = set()
    cleaned_categories = []
    for cat in categories:
        cat_lower = cat.lower()
        if cat_lower not in seen:
            seen.add(cat_lower)
            cleaned_categories.append(cat)
    
    return cleaned_categories

# Test extractions

for i in range(min(5, len(businesses_all))):

}

}
